factors.summary: The company is maintaining its current debt levels, with no indication of deleveraging or increased borrowing beyond its existing Revolving Facility usage.

evidence.text: Our Revolving Facility will be primarily used for working capital, general corporate purposes and funding capital expenditures and growth opportunities. In addition, we may use borrowings under the Revolving Facility to facilitate debt repayment and consolidation.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable, supported by positive operating cash flows and manageable investing activities.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities was $654.4 million for the three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to $179.8 million for the same period in 2022. The company used $178.9 million in cash for investing activities.

factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating, particularly in the single-family homebuilding market, due to rising interest rates and inflation.

evidence.text: Rising interest rates and inflation have dampened, and may continue to dampen, near term housing industry demand as homes become less affordable for consumers, investors and builders.

factors.summary: Order backlog is not explicitly mentioned, but net sales have decreased, indicating potential softness in demand.

evidence.text: Net sales for the first quarter of 2023 decreased 31.6% from the same period last year.

factors.summary: Pricing power is stable with a focus on passing on material costs and maintaining margins through improved product mix.

evidence.text: Our gross margin percentage increased...primarily attributable to an improved product mix toward our value-add products...and relative sales strength against decreased housing starts.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin value-add products, benefiting the company.

evidence.text: Our gross margin percentage increased...primarily attributable to an improved product mix toward our value-add products due to our recent strategic investments in multi-family value-add operations.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, primarily due to declining housing starts.

evidence.text: Core organic sales, primarily in the single family customer segment, decreased by 26.3%.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining across all product categories except windows, doors, and millwork, which saw slight growth.

evidence.text: We experienced decreased net sales in all of our product categories, except windows, doors, and millwork...primarily due to a decline in core organic sales as a result of declining housing starts as well as commodity deflation.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2023 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2022

factors.summary: The single-family homebuilding market is declining, while the repair and remodel market is expected to grow slightly.

evidence.text: While influenced by housing starts to a lesser degree than the homebuilding market, the repair and remodel end market is still dependent upon some of the same factors...we expect that our ability to remain competitive in this space will depend on our continued ability to provide a high level of customer service coupled with a broad product and digital offerings.

factors.summary: Raw material costs are experiencing deflation, which is impacting net sales negatively but improving gross margins.

evidence.text: Commodity price deflation accounted for another 11.8% decrease in net sales.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended March 31, 2023 Compared with the Three Months Ended March 31, 2022

factors.summary: Gross margins are improving due to better product mix and decreased housing starts.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage increased...primarily attributable to an improved product mix toward our value-add products...and relative sales strength against decreased housing starts.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its market share and expects to increase it through strategic investments and acquisitions.

evidence.text: We believe we are well-positioned to take advantage of favorable long-term industry trends and to increase our market share, both organically and through acquisitions.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are acting as a headwind due to rising interest rates and inflation, making housing less affordable.

evidence.text: Rising interest rates and inflation have dampened, and may continue to dampen, near term housing industry demand as homes become less affordable for consumers, investors and builders.

factors.summary: Construction activity is slowing, particularly in the single-family homebuilding sector.

evidence.text: Actual U.S. total housing starts were 0.3 million for the first quarter of 2023, a decrease of 17.9% compared to the first quarter of 2022.

factors.summary: The company is active in acquisitions, with recent deals expanding its market presence.

evidence.text: During the period, we completed the business combination of Noltex for a total purchase price of $84.6 million.
